question.text: The stamp duty rate for buyers of equity futures and equity options contracts is:

answer.text: 0.002% (Rs. 200 per crore) for futures buyers and 0.003% (Rs. 300 per crore) for options buyers
0.01% for both futures and options buyers
No stamp duty is applicable on derivative contracts

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Stamp duty on equity derivatives: 0.002% (Rs. 200 per crore) for futures buyers and 0.003% (Rs. 300 per crore) for options buyers. This is in addition to STT, GST, and transaction charges. **The Trap:** Stamp duty applies only to buyers, not sellers. The rates are different for futures and options. **Cross-Exam Context:** Trading costs including stamp duty are part of the regulatory framework. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Stamp duty: Futures 0.002%, Options 0.003%. Both buyer-side only. SEBI turnover fees: Rs. 10 per crore + GST.
